channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Nonequilibrium ordering dynamics of confined soft alginate hydrogel colloids driven by time-evolving electrostatic interactions
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 09 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70266-w

Elucidating the dynamics of non-equilibrium orders remains a challenge. Here, the authors show that time-varying electrostatic interactions drive confined alginate hydrogels to self-organize into tunable, hexagonally ordered lattices.
item.dc:title: Nonequilibrium ordering dynamics of confined soft alginate hydrogel colloids driven by time-evolving electrostatic interactions
item.dc:creator: In Hwan Jung
Chetan C. Revadekar
Hag Sung Lee
Hyerim Hwang
Hyosung An
Bum Jun Park

item.title: Kibble-Zurek mechanism and beyond in a holographic superfluid disk
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 09 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69940-w

The Kibble-Zurek mechanism describes universal scaling laws in the dynamics across a continuous or quantum phase transition driven in finite time, but it is limited to slow quenches. Here, by focusing on a holographic superfluid disk model, the authors advance beyond this framework and uncover universal statistics of topological defects that holds for both slow and fast quenches.
item.dc:title: Kibble-Zurek mechanism and beyond in a holographic superfluid disk
item.dc:creator: Chuan-Yin Xia
Hua-Bi Zeng
András Grabarits
Adolfo del Campo
